rules.name: Hyper Adaptions
rules.effect:
At the start of the first battle round, select one of the following Hyper-adaptations to be active for Tyranids units from your army until the end of the battle:

SWARMING INSTINCTS
Each time a Tyranids model with this Hyper-adaptation makes an attack that targets an enemy Infantry or Swarm unit, that attack has the [SUSTAINED HITS 1] ability.


HYPER-AGGRESSION 
Each time a Tyranids model with this Hyper-adaptation makes an attack that targets an enemy Monster or Vehicle unit, that attack has the [LETHAL HITS] ability.

HIVE PREDATORS 
Each time a Tyranids model with this Hyper-adaptation makes an attack that targets an enemy Character unit, if a Critical Hit is scored, that attack has the [PRECISION] ability.

stratagems.name: Rapid Regeneration
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Shooting phase or the Fight phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, models in your unit have the Feel No Pain 6+ ability. If your unit is within Synapse Range of your army, models in your unit have the Feel No Pain 5+ ability instead.
stratagems.summary: Feel No Pain 6+; Feel No Pain 5+ if within Synapse Range

stratagems.name: Adrenal Surge
stratagems.timing: Fight phase.
stratagems.cond: Up to two TYRANIDS units from your army that are within Synapse Range of your army and are eligible to fight, or one other TYRANIDS unit from your army that is eligible to fight.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, each time a model in any of those selected units makes an attack, an unmodified Hit roll of 5+ scores a Critical Hit.
stratagems.summary: [CRITICAL HITS] on Hit rolls of 5+

stratagems.name: Death Frenzy
stratagems.timing: Fight phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit is destroyed, if that model has not fought this phase, roll one D6: on a 4+, do not remove it from play. The destroyed model can fight after the attacking model’s unit has finished making its attacks, and is then removed from play.
stratagems.summary: Each destroyed model not yet fought this phase: roll D6, on 4+ stays on table to fight before removal

stratagems.name: Overrun
stratagems.timing: Fight phase, just before a TYRANIDS unit from your army Consolidates.
stratagems.cond: That TYRANIDS unit.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, each time your unit Consolidates, models in it can move an additional 3" as long as your unit can end that move within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units. If your unit is within Synapse Range of your army and not within Engagement Range of any enemy units, instead of making that Consolidation move, it can make a Normal move of up to 6".
stratagems.summary: Consolidate +3" if ending in Engagement Range; or 6" Normal move if in Synapse Range and not in Engagement Range

stratagems.name: Predatory Imperative
stratagems.timing: Your Command phase.
stratagems.cond: Up to two TYRANIDS units from your army that are within Synapse Range of your army, or one other TYRANIDS unit from your army.
stratagems.effect: Select one Hyper-adaptation. Until the start of your next Command phase, that Hyper-adaptation is active for those selected units in addition to any other that may be active for your army.
stratagems.summary: Activate additional Hyper-adaptation until start of next Command phase

stratagems.name: Endless Swarm
stratagems.timing: Your Command phase.
stratagems.cond: Up to two ENDLESS MULTITUDE units from your army that are within Synapse Range of your army, or one other ENDLESS MULTITUDE unit from your army.
stratagems.effect: You can return up to D3+3 destroyed models to each of the selected units.
stratagems.summary: Return up to D3+3 destroyed models to each selected unit

stratagems.name: Rapid Regeneration
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Shooting phase or the Fight phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, models in your unit have the Feel No Pain 6+ ability. If your unit is within Synapse Range of your army, models in your unit have the Feel No Pain 5+ ability instead.
stratagems.summary: Feel No Pain 6+; Feel No Pain 5+ if within Synapse Range

rules.name: Feed the Swarm
rules.effect:
In your Command phase, each HARVESTER unit from your army can Regenerate one friendly TYRANIDS unit that is within 6" of it. A unit can only be regenerated once per phase. Each time a unit regenerates do one of the following:

- One model in that unit regains up to D3+1 lost wounds
- One destroyed INFANTRY model (excluding CHARACTERS) is returned to that unit with its full wounds. If that unit is an ENDLESS MULTITUDE unit, return up to 3 destroyed models are returned instead.

stratagems.name: Broodguard Impulse
stratagems.timing: Any phase.
stratagems.cond: One HARVESTER unit from your army that was just destroyed. You can use this Stratagem on that unit even though it was just destroyed.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the battle, each time a friendly TYRANIDS model makes an attack that targets the enemy unit that just destroyed your HARVESTER unit, add 1 to the Wound roll.
stratagems.summary: +1 to Wound rolls against the unit that destroyed your HARVESTER, for rest of battle

stratagems.name: Reclaim Biomass
stratagems.timing: Any phase, when a TYRANIDS unit from your army is destroyed, before the last model in it is removed from play.
stratagems.cond: One HARVESTER unit from your army that is within 6" of that destroyed unit.
stratagems.effect: Regenerate one friendly TYRANIDS unit within 6" of your HARVESTER unit (See Feed the Swarm).
stratagems.summary: Regenerate one friendly TYRANIDS unit within 6" of your HARVESTER unit

stratagems.name: Tyrannoformed
stratagems.timing: Command phase.
stratagems.cond: One HARVESTER unit from your army that is within range of an objective marker you control.
stratagems.effect: That objective marker remains under your control, even if you have no models within range of it, until your opponent controls it at the start or end of any turn.
stratagems.summary: Objective marker stays under your control even without models in range until opponent controls it

stratagems.name: Ablative Carapace
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Shooting phase or the Fight phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets.
stratagems.cond: One HARVESTER unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, models in your unit have the Feel No Pain 5+ ability. If your unit is within range of an objective marker you control, until the end of the phase models in your unit have the Feel No Pain 4+ ability instead.
stratagems.summary: Feel No Pain 5+ (4+ if within range of controlled objective)

stratagems.name: Secure Biomass
stratagems.timing: Fight phase.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS unit from your army that has not been selected to fight this phase.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, melee weapons equipped by models in your unit have the [LETHAL HITS] ability. If your unit is a HARVESTER unit, each time a model in that unit makes a melee attack, a successful unmodified Hit roll of 5+ scores a Critical Hit as well.
stratagems.summary: Grant [LETHAL HITS] to unit's melee weapons; if HARVESTER, unmodified Hit rolls of 5+ also score Critical Hits

stratagems.name: Rapacious Hunger
stratagems.timing: Your Fight phase.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS unit from your army that just destroyed an enemy unit.
stratagems.effect: Your unit immediately Regenerates (See Feed the Swarm). When doing so, if your unit is a HARVESTER unit and you choose for one model to regain up to D3 lost wounds, that model regains up to 3 lost wounds instead.
stratagems.summary: Unit Regenerates immediately; HARVESTER units regain 3 wounds instead of D3 for one model

stratagems.name: Reclaim Biomass
stratagems.timing: Any phase, when a TYRANIDS unit from your army is destroyed, before the last model in it is removed from play.
stratagems.cond: One HARVESTER unit from your army that is within 6" of that destroyed unit.
stratagems.effect: Regenerate one friendly TYRANIDS unit within 6" of your HARVESTER unit (See Feed the Swarm).
stratagems.summary: Regenerate one friendly TYRANIDS unit within 6" of your HARVESTER unit

rules.name: Enraged Behemoths
rules.effect: Each time a TYRANIDS MONSTER model from your army makes an attack, add 1 to the Hit roll if that model’s unit is below its Starting Strength, and add 1 to the Wound roll as well if that model’s unit is Below Half-strength. In addition, while a TYRANIDS MONSTER unit from your army (excluding Battle-shocked units) is at its Starting Strength, add 2 to the Objective Control characteristic of models in that unit.

stratagems.name: Corrosive Viscera
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Shooting phase or the Fight phase, just after a TYRANIDS MONSTER model from your army with the Deadly Demise ability that cannot FLY is destroyed.
stratagems.cond: That TYRANIDS MONSTER model. You can use this Stratagem on that model even though it was just destroyed.
stratagems.effect: Do not roll one D6 to determine whether mortal wounds are inflicted by your model’s Deadly Demise ability. Instead, mortal wounds are automatically inflicted.
stratagems.summary: Deadly Demise auto-inflicts mortal wounds; no roll needed

stratagems.name: Rampaging Monstrosities
stratagems.timing: Fight phase.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS MONSTER unit from your army that has not been selected to fight this phase.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit makes an attack, you can re-roll the Hit roll.
stratagems.summary: Re-roll Hit rolls for unit's attacks until end of phase

stratagems.name: Savage Roar
stratagems.timing: Fight phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS MONSTER unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks.
stratagems.effect: That enemy unit must take a Battle-shock test and, until the end of the phase, each time a model in that enemy unit makes an attack that targets your unit, subtract 1 from the Hit roll. If that Battle-shock test was failed, subtract 1 from the Wound roll as well.
stratagems.summary: Enemy unit takes Battle-shock test; -1 Hit rolls vs your unit until end of phase; if failed, also -1 Wound rolls

stratagems.name: Untrammelled Ferocity
stratagems.timing: Your Movement phase.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS MONSTER unit from your army that has not been selected to move this phase.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit makes a Normal, Advance or Fall Back move, it can move through models (excluding TITANIC models) and sections of terrain features that are 4" or less in height. When doing so: It can move within Engagement Range of enemy models, but cannot end that move within Engagement Range of them. It can also move through sections of terrain features that are more than 4" in height, but if it does, after its unit has moved, roll one D6: on a 1, your unit is Battle-shocked.
stratagems.summary: Move through non-TITANIC models and terrain ≤4"; can move within but not end in Engagement Range of enemies; can move through terrain >4" (roll D6 on 1 becomes Battle-shocked)

stratagems.name: Swarm-guided Salvoes
stratagems.timing: Your Shooting phase.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS MONSTER unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot this phase.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, ranged weapons equipped by models in your unit have the [IGNORES COVER] ability, and until the end of the phase each time a model in your unit makes an attack, you can ignore any or all modifiers to that model’s Ballistic Skill characteristic and any or all modifiers to the Hit roll.
stratagems.summary: [IGNORES COVER] on unit's ranged weapons; ignore BS and Hit roll modifiers on attacks

stratagems.name: Massive Impact
stratagems.timing: Your Charge phase, just after a TYRANIDS MONSTER model from your army ends a Charge move.
stratagems.cond: That TYRANIDS MONSTER model.
stratagems.effect: Select one enemy unit within Engagement Range of your model and roll six D6: for each 4+, that enemy unit suffers 1 mortal wound.
stratagems.summary: Roll 6D6 vs enemy unit in Engagement Range; each 4+ inflicts 1 mortal wound

rules.name: Synaptic Imperitives
rules.effect:
At the start of the battle round, you can select one of the Synaptic Imperatives shown below. Until the end of the battle round, that Synaptic Imperative is active for your army and while a TYRANIDS unit from your army is within Synapse Range of your army, it will benefit from it. Each Synaptic Imperative can only be selected once per battle.


- Synaptic Augmentation: While this unit is within Synapse Range of your army, models in this unit have a 5+ invulnerable save.
- Surging Vitality: While this unit is within Synapse Range of your army, add 1 to Advance and Charge rolls made for this unit.
- Goaded to Slaughter: While this unit is within Synapse Range of your army, each time a model in this unit makes a melee attack, add 1 to the Hit roll

stratagems.name: The Smothering Shadow
stratagems.timing: Any phase, just after an enemy unit fails a Battle-shock test.
stratagems.cond: One SYNAPSE unit from your army within 12" of that enemy unit.
stratagems.effect: Roll six D6: for each 3+, that enemy unit suffers 1 mortal wound.
stratagems.summary: Roll 6D6; each 3+ inflicts 1 mortal wound on target enemy unit

stratagems.name: Synaptic Channelling
stratagems.timing: Command phase.
stratagems.cond: One SYNAPSE unit from your army.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the turn, while a friendly TYRANIDS unit is within 9" of the selected unit, that unit is within Synapse Range of your army.
stratagems.summary: While friendly TYRANIDS unit within 9", selected unit is within Synapse Range

stratagems.name: Irresistible Will
stratagems.timing: Your Shooting phase or the Fight phase.
stratagems.cond: One SYNAPSE unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot or fight this phase, and one enemy unit within 24" of and visible to the SYNAPSE unit.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, each time a friendly TYRANIDS model makes an attack that targets that enemy unit, if the attacking model’s unit is within 6" of your SYNAPSE unit, re-roll a Hit roll of 1 and re-roll a Wound roll of 1.
stratagems.summary: Re-roll Hit rolls of 1 and Wound rolls of 1 for TYRANIDS attacks within 6" of SYNAPSE unit

stratagems.name: Reinforced Hive Node
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Shooting phase or the Fight phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets.
stratagems.cond: One SYNAPSE unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks.
stratagems.effect: Until the attacking unit has finished making its attacks, each time an attack targets your unit, worsen the Armour Penetration characteristic of that attack by 1.
stratagems.summary: -1 AP on incoming attacks

stratagems.name: Imperative Dominance
stratagems.timing: Your Command phase.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS unit from your army that is within Synapse Range of your army.
stratagems.effect: Select one Synaptic Imperative, even if you have already selected that imperative this battle. Until the start of your next Command phase, that Synaptic Imperative is active for your unit instead of any other Synaptic Imperative that is active for your army.
stratagems.summary: Select a Synaptic Imperative; it's active for this unit instead of army-wide imperative until start of next Command phase

stratagems.name: Override Instincts
stratagems.timing: Your Movement phase.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS unit from your army that is within Synapse Range of your army and made a Fall Back move this phase.
stratagems.effect: Your unit is eligible to shoot and declare a charge this turn.
stratagems.summary: Eligible to shoot and declare charge this turn

rules.name: Insurmountable Odds
rules.effect: Each time an enemy unit is selected to shoot, after that unit has finished making its attacks, if one or more models from one or more ENDLESS MULTITUDE units from your army were destroyed as a result of those attacks, each such unit can make a Surge move. To do so, roll one D6: that unit can be moved a distance in inches up to the result, but that unit must end that move as close as possible to the closest enemy unit (excluding AIRCRAFT). When doing so, those models can be moved within Engagement Range of enemy units. A unit cannot make a Surge Move while Battle-shocked

stratagems.name: Synaptic Goading
stratagems.timing: Any phase, just before an ENDLESS MULTITUDE unit from your army that is within Synapse Range of your army makes a Surge move.
stratagems.cond: That ENDLESS MULTITUDE unit.
stratagems.effect: When making that Surge move, you can re-roll the D6 to determine how far your unit moves, and your unit can end that move as close as possible to the closest objective marker (instead of as close as possible to the closest enemy unit]. All other rules for making Surge moves still apply.
stratagems.summary: Re-roll Surge move distance; move toward closest objective instead of closest enemy

stratagems.name: Unending Waves
stratagems.timing: Any phase.
stratagems.cond: One ENDLESS MULTITUDE unit from your army that was just destroyed. You can use this Stratagem on that unit even though it was just destroyed.
stratagems.effect: Add a new unit to your army identical to your destroyed unit, in Strategic Reserves, at its Starting Strength.
stratagems.summary: Add destroyed unit to Strategic Reserves at Starting Strength

stratagems.name: Teeming Masses
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Shooting phase or the Fight phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets.
stratagems.cond: One ENDLESS MULTITUDE unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, each time an attack targets your unit, subtract 1 from the Hit roll.
stratagems.summary: -1 to Hit rolls on all incoming attacks

stratagems.name: Swarming Masses
stratagems.timing: Your Shooting phase or the Fight phase.
stratagems.cond: One ENDLESS MULTITUDE unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot or fight this phase.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, weapons equipped by models in your unit have the [SUSTAINED HITS 1] ability, and If your unit contains 15 or more models, each time a model in your unit makes an attack, an unmodified Hit roll of 5+ scores a Critical Hit.
stratagems.summary: Grant [SUSTAINED HITS 1] to unit's weapons; if 15+ models, unmodified 5+ hits are Critical Hits

stratagems.name: Bounding Advance
stratagems.timing: Your Movement phase.
stratagems.cond: One ENDLESS MULTITUDE unit from your army.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, each time your unit Advances, do not make an Advance roll. Instead, until the end of the phase, add 6" to the Move characteristic of models in your unit.
stratagems.summary: Unit doesn't roll to Advance; +6" Move instead

stratagems.name: Preservation Imperative
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Shooting phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets.
stratagems.cond: One ENDLESS MULTITUDE unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, your unit is treated as containing fewer than five models for the purpose of the [BLAST] ability.
stratagems.summary: Treat unit as <5 models for [BLAST] purposes

stratagems.name: Synaptic Goading
stratagems.timing: Any phase, just before an ENDLESS MULTITUDE unit from your army that is within Synapse Range of your army makes a Surge move.
stratagems.cond: That ENDLESS MULTITUDE unit.
stratagems.effect: When making that Surge move, you can re-roll the D6 to determine how far your unit moves, and your unit can end that move as close as possible to the closest objective marker (instead of as close as possible to the closest enemy unit]. All other rules for making Surge moves still apply.
stratagems.summary: Re-roll Surge move distance; move toward closest objective instead of closest enemy

rules.name: Questing Tendrils
rules.effect: TYRANIDS units with this ability are eligible to charge in a turn in which they Fell Back. Vanguard Invader units with this ability are eligible to charge in a turn in which they Advanced.

rules.name: Vanguard Prime
rules.effect: DEATHLEAPER loses Hunter Organism rule and can be your WARLORD

stratagems.name: Surprise Assault
stratagems.timing: Your Shooting phase or the Fight phase, just after a VANGUARD INVADER unit from your army has selected its targets.
stratagems.cond: That VANGUARD INVADER unit.
stratagems.effect: Select one enemy unit that was selected as the target of one or more of your unit’s attacks. That enemy unit must take a Battle-shock test. Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit makes an attack that targets that enemy unit, add 1 to the Hit roll. If the Battle-shock test was failed, add 1 to the Wound roll as well.
stratagems.summary: Target enemy unit takes Battle-shock test; +1 Hit rolls against it this phase; +1 Wound rolls if test failed

stratagems.name: Assassin Beasts
stratagems.timing: Fight phase.
stratagems.cond: One VANGUARD INVADER INFANTRY unit from your army that has not been selected to fight this phase.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, melee weapons equipped by models in your unit have the [PRECISION] ability.
stratagems.summary: Grant [PRECISION] to unit's melee weapons

stratagems.name: Seeded Broods
stratagems.timing: Your Movement phase.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS unit from your army that is in Reserves, or up to two VANGUARD INVADER units from your army that are in Reserves.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, for the purposes of setting up those selected units on the battlefield, treat the current battle round number as being one higher than it actually is.
stratagems.summary: Treat current battle round as +1 for setup purposes

stratagems.name: Hypersensory Scillia
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Movement phase, just after an enemy unit ends a Normal, Advance or Fall Back move.
stratagems.cond: Up to two VANGUARD INVADER units from your army that are within 9" of that enemy unit, or one other TYRANIDS INFANTRY unit from your army that is within 9" of that enemy unit.
stratagems.effect: Those selected units can each make a Normal move of up to 6".
stratagems.summary: Those units move up to 6"

stratagems.name: Unseen Lurkers
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Shooting phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets.
stratagems.cond: One VANGUARD INVADER unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, your unit can only be selected as the target of a ranged attack if the attacking model is within 18" or, if your unit has the Lone Operative ability, if the attacking model is within 6". Your opponent can select new targets for the attacking unit’s attacks.
stratagems.summary: Unit can't be targeted by ranged attacks beyond 18" (or 6" if Lone Operative); opponent can reselect targets

stratagems.name: Invisible Hunter
stratagems.timing: End of your opponent’s Fight phase.
stratagems.cond: Up to two VANGUARD INVADER units from your army, or one TYRANIDS INFANTRY unit from your army.
stratagems.effect: Remove the targeted units from the battlefield and place them into Strategic Reserves.
stratagems.summary: Remove targeted units; place into Strategic Reserves

stratagems.name: Unseen Lurkers
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Shooting phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets.
stratagems.cond: One VANGUARD INVADER unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, your unit can only be selected as the target of a ranged attack if the attacking model is within 18" or, if your unit has the Lone Operative ability, if the attacking model is within 6". Your opponent can select new targets for the attacking unit’s attacks.
stratagems.summary: Unit can't be targeted by ranged attacks beyond 18" (or 6" if Lone Operative); opponent can reselect targets

rules.name: Leader-beasts
rules.effect: TYRANID WARRIORS and WINGED TYRANID PRIME units from your army have a 5+ invulnerable save.

rules.name: Keywords
rules.effect: TYRANID WARRIORS WITH RANGED BIO‑WEAPONS and TYRANID WARRIORS WITH MELEE BIO‑WEAPONS units from your army gain the TYRANID WARRIORS and BATTLELINE keywords, and while such a unit is not Battle‑shocked, TYRANID WARRIORS models in that unit have an Objective Control characteristic of 3.

stratagems.name: Synaptic Amplification
stratagems.timing: Your Shooting phase or the Fight phase.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot or fight this phase.
stratagems.effect: If that unit is a TYRANID WARRIORS unit, select up to one ENDLESS MULTITUDE unit from your army, that is not Battle-shocked, within 6" of it. Until the end of the phase, each time a model in those units makes an attack, re-roll a Wound roll of 1, and, if it is a TYRANID WARRIORS unit, re-roll a Hit roll of 1 as well.
stratagems.summary: TYRANID WARRIORS unit grants nearby ENDLESS MULTITUDE unit re-roll Wound rolls of 1 (or Hit rolls of 1 if target is also TYRANID WARRIORS) until end of phase

stratagems.name: Spontaneous Hypercorrosion
stratagems.timing: Your Shooting phase or the Fight phase.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot or fight this phase.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, add 2 to the Strength characteristic of ranged weapons equipped by models in your unit and add 1 to the Strength characteristic of melee weapons equipped by TYRANID WARRIORS and WINGED TYRANID PRIME models in your unit.
stratagems.summary: Ranged weapons: +2 Str. TYRANID WARRIORS and WINGED TYRANID PRIME melee weapons: +1 Str

stratagems.name: Restorative Impulse
stratagems.timing: Your Command phase.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANID WARRIORS unit from your army that is below its Starting Strength.
stratagems.effect: Return 1 destroyed model (excluding CHARACTER models) to your unit.
stratagems.summary: Return 1 destroyed non-CHARACTER model to unit

stratagems.name: Synaptic Micronodes
stratagems.timing: Your Movement phase.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANID WARRIORS unit from your army.
stratagems.effect: Select one objective marker you control that your unit is within range of. That objective marker remains under your control until your opponent’s Level of Control over that objective marker is greater than yours at the end of a phase.
stratagems.summary: Objective marker stays controlled until opponent's Level of Control exceeds yours

stratagems.name: Parasitic Payload
stratagems.timing: Your Shooting phase.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANID WARRIORS WITH RANGED BIO-WEAPONS unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot this phase.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, ranged weapons equipped by models in your unit have the [IGNORES COVER] ability. After your unit has resolved its shooting attacks this phase, select one enemy unit hit by one or more of those attacks. Until the end of the turn, models in that unit cannot have the Benefit of Cover.
stratagems.summary: Grant [IGNORES COVER] to unit's ranged weapons; after shooting, selected hit enemy unit loses Benefit of Cover until end of turn

stratagems.name: Synaptic Shield
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Shooting phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANID WARRIORS unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks.
stratagems.effect: If that unit is a TYRANID WARRIORS unit, select up to one ENDLESS MULTITUDE unit from your army, that is not Battle-shocked, within 6" of it. Until the end of the phase, each time a ranged attack targets one of those units, if the Strength characteristic of that attack is greater than the Toughness characteristic of that unit, subtract 1 from the Wound roll.
stratagems.summary: If TYRANID WARRIORS, select one ENDLESS MULTITUDE unit within 6" (not Battle-shocked); -1 to Wound rolls if attack Strength > target Toughness

stratagems.name: Adaptive Optimisation
stratagems.timing: Command phase.
stratagems.cond: One MAWLOC or TRYGON unit from your army.
stratagems.effect: Until the start of your next Command phase, your unit has the SYNAPSE keyword.
stratagems.summary: Gain SYNAPSE keyword until next Command phase

stratagems.name: Replenishing Swarms
stratagems.timing: Your Movement phase.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS unit from your army, wholly within 9" of one or more Tunnel Markers you placed.
stratagems.effect: One model in your unit regains up to D3+1 lost wounds, or you can return up to D3+1 destroyed models with a Wounds characteristic of 1 to your unit, with their full wounds remaining, instead.
stratagems.summary: Heal D3+1 wounds on one model OR return D3+1 destroyed 1-wound models

stratagems.name: Enfilading Emergence
stratagems.timing: End of your Movement phase.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS unit from your army that was set up as Reinforcements this turn.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of your next Fight phase, weapons equipped by models in your unit have the [SUSTAINED HITS 1] and [IGNORES COVER] abilities.
stratagems.summary: Grant [SUSTAINED HITS 1] and [IGNORES COVER] to unit's weapons until end of next Fight phase

stratagems.name: Tunnel Network
stratagems.timing: End of your Movement phase.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS unit from your army that is wholly within 9" of one or more of your Tunnel Markers and not within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units.
stratagems.effect: Remove your unit from the battlefield and set it up again, wholly within 9" of another Tunnel Marker you placed, and more than 6" horizontally away from all enemy units.
stratagems.summary: Remove unit from battlefield; redeploy wholly within 9" of a Tunnel Marker, >6" from all enemies

stratagems.name: Swarming Assault
stratagems.timing: Your Charge phase.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS MONSTER unit from your army that was set up as Reinforcements this turn.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, friendly TYRANIDS units within 6" of your unit can re-roll Charge rolls.
stratagems.summary: Friendly TYRANIDS within 6" re-roll Charge rolls

stratagems.name: Retreat Below
stratagems.timing: End of your opponent’s Fight phase.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS unit or up to two BURROWER units from your army that are not within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units.
stratagems.effect: Remove your unit from the battlefield and place it into Strategic Reserves.
stratagems.summary: Remove unit from battlefield; place into Strategic Reserves

stratagems.name: Bio-acid Surge
stratagems.timing: Fight phase.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS unit from your army that is within Synapse Range of your army and has not been selected to fight this phase.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, melee weapons equipped by models in your unit have the [SUSTAINED HITS 1] ability.
stratagems.summary: Grant [SUSTAINED HITS 1] to unit's melee weapons

stratagems.name: Phagic Spores
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Shooting phase or the Fight phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANID WARRIORS WITH MELEE BIO-WEAPONS or TYRANID WARRIORS WITH RANGED BIO-WEAPONS unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit is destroyed as a result of an attack, do not remove it from play; after the attacking unit has finished making its attacks, roll one D6: on a 3-5, the closest enemy unit within 3" of and visible to your unit suffers 1 mortal wound; on a 6, the closest enemy unit within 3" of and visible to your unit suffers 2 mortal wounds. The destroyed model is then removed from play.
stratagems.summary: On model destruction, roll D6: 3-5 inflicts 1 mortal wound to closest visible enemy unit within 3"; 6 inflicts 2 mortal wounds. Model removed after attacks finish

stratagems.name: Expendable Biomass
stratagems.summary: The Tyranids will expend the lives of their lesser organisms in droves provided it results in their prey’s defeat.

stratagems.name: Hive Sight
stratagems.timing: Your Charge phase.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS SYNAPSE unit from your army and one friendly TYRANIDS unit that has not declared a charge this phase.
stratagems.effect: Select one enemy unit that is visible to your SYNAPSE unit. Until the end of the phase, each time your other unit declares a charge, it can include that enemy unit as a target of that charge, even if it is not visible to it, and when doing so, add 1 to the Charge roll.
stratagems.summary: Select visible enemy unit; other units gain +1 to Charge rolls against it and can charge it even if not visible

stratagems.name: Lithe Killers
stratagems.timing: Fight phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, models in your unit have a 5+ invulnerable save.
stratagems.summary: Grant 5+ invulnerable save to unit

stratagems.name: Adrenalised Slaughter
stratagems.timing: Fight phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit is destroyed, if that model has not fought this phase, roll one D6. On a 5+, do not remove the destroyed model from play; it can fight after the attacking unit has finished making its attacks, and is then removed from play.
stratagems.summary: Destroyed model fights on a 5+ if hasn't fought this phase, then removed

stratagems.name: Talon-tip Swarm
stratagems.timing: Fight phase, just after a TYRANIDS unit from your army destroys an enemy unit.
stratagems.cond: That TYRANIDS unit.
stratagems.effect: If your unit is not within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units, it can make a Normal move of up to 3". After that move, if your unit is within range of an objective marker and no enemy units are within range of that objective marker, that objective marker is Secured by your army.
stratagems.summary: Move up to 3" if not in Engagement Range; Secure objective if within range and no enemies nearby

stratagems.name: Predatory Pounce
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Movement phase, just after an enemy unit opens a Hatchway.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS unit from your army that is within 3" of that Hatchway and is not within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units.
stratagems.effect: Your unit can make a Pounce move. To do so, your unit can be moved up to 3", but must end that move as close as possible to that Hatchway. When doing so, those models can be moved within Engagement Range of that enemy unit, but only that enemy unit.
stratagems.summary: Unit moves up to 3" toward Hatchway, can end in Engagement Range of one enemy unit

stratagems.name: Living Avalanche
stratagems.timing: Your Movement phase, just after a TYRANIDS unit from your army has opened a Hatchway.
stratagems.cond: That TYRANIDS unit.
stratagems.effect: Your unit can make a Swarm move. To do so, your unit can be moved up to 3", but at least one model from your unit must end that move on the opposite side of that Hatchway from the one it started the phase on. When doing so, those models can be moved within Engagement Range of enemy units.
stratagems.summary: Unit moves up to 3" through a Hatchway; at least one model must cross it; can move into Engagement Range of enemies

stratagems.name: Squirming Masses
stratagems.timing: Your Movement phase, at the start of the Reinforcements step.
stratagems.cond: One ENDLESS MULTITUDE unit from your army that is in Reserves.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, models in your unit have the Deep Strike ability and, when setting them upon the battlefield using that ability, they can be set up anywhere on the battlefield that is more than 6" horizontally away from all enemy units, provided they are also within Synapse Range of your army.
stratagems.summary: Models gain Deep Strike; set up anywhere >6" from enemies, within Synapse Range

stratagems.name: Swarm Hunters
stratagems.timing: Your Shooting phase.
stratagems.cond: One TERMAGANTS unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot this phase.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, models in your unit can ignore other models in their unit for the purposes of determining visibility. In addition, if your unit is within Synapse Range of your army, each time a model in your unit makes an attack, you can re-roll the Hit roll.
stratagems.summary: Models ignore own unit for visibility; re-roll Hit rolls if within Synapse Range

stratagems.name: Onrushing Horde
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Shooting phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets.
stratagems.cond: One TYRANIDS unit from your army (excluding WINGED TYRANID PRIME units), that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks.

stratagems.summary: -1 to Hit rolls on all incoming attacks

stratagems.name: Hyperadrenal Reflexes
stratagems.timing: Fight phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets.
stratagems.cond: One GREAT DEVOURER unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, models in your unit have a 4+ invulnerable save.
stratagems.summary: Grant 4+ invulnerable save to unit

stratagems.name: Pervasive Dread

stratagems.cond: That GREAT DEVOURER unit.
stratagems.effect: In your opponent’s next Command phase, each enemy unit on the battlefield must take a Battle-shock test, subtracting 1 from the result. This Stratagem cannot cause a unit to take two Battle-shock tests in the same phase.
stratagems.summary: Enemy units take Battle-shock test at -1 in opponent's next Command phase

stratagems.name: Hunting Grounds
stratagems.timing: Your Movement phase, just after a GREAT DEVOURER unit from your army opens a Hatchway.
stratagems.cond: That GREAT DEVOURER unit.
stratagems.effect: Your unit can make a Normal move of up to 3", provided at least one of its models ends that move on the opposite side of that Hatchway from when your unit opened it.
stratagems.summary: Unit moves up to 3" through opened Hatchway; at least one model must end on opposite side

stratagems.name: Outflank
stratagems.timing: End of your opponent’s Fight phase.
stratagems.cond: One GENESTEALERS or PURESTRAIN GENESTEALERS unit from your army that is wholly within an Entry Zone.
stratagems.effect: Remove your unit from the battlefield and place it into Strategic Reserves. It will arrive back on the battlefield in the Reinforcements step of your next Movement phase. When doing so, if it was within one of your Entry Zones when it was removed, it must be set up wholly within another one of your Entry Zones. If it was within one of your opponent’s Entry Zones when it was removed, it must be set up wholly within another one of your opponent’s Entry Zones instead. If this is not possible, your unit is destroyed.
stratagems.summary: Remove unit to Strategic Reserves; return in next Movement phase within an Entry Zone (same side if removed from your zone, opposite side if removed from opponent's zone, or destroyed)

glossary.name: Deadly Demise
glossary.effect: Some models have 'Deadly Demise x' listed in their abilities. When such a model is destroyed, roll one D6 before removing it from play (if such a model is a TRANSPORT, roll before any embarked models disembark). On a 6, each unit within 6" of that model suffers a number of mortal wounds denoted by 'x' (if this is a random number, roll separately for each unit within 6").

glossary.name: Deep Strike
glossary.effect:
During the Declare Battle Formations step, if every model in a unit has this ability, you can set it up in Reserves instead of setting it up on the battlefield. If you do, in the Reinforcements step of one of your Movement phases you can set up this unit anywhere on the battlefield that is more than 9" horizontally away from all enemy models.

If a unit with the Deep Strike ability arrives from Strategic Reserves, the controlling player can choose for that unit to be set up either using the rules for Strategic Reserves or using the Deep Strike ability.

glossary.name: Shadow in the Warp
glossary.effect: If your Army Faction is TYRANIDS, once per battle, in either player’s Command phase, if one or more units from your army with this ability are on the battlefield, you can unleash the Shadow in the Warp. When you do, each enemy unit on the battlefield must take a Battle-shock test. Each time an enemy unit takes such a Battle-shock test, if it is within 6" of one or more Synapse units from your army, subtract 1 from that test.

glossary.name: Synapse
glossary.effect:
If your Army Faction is TYRANIDS, while a TYRANIDS unit from your army is within 6" of one or more friendly SYNAPSE  models, that TYRANIDS unit is said to be within Synapse Range of that model and of your army. While a Tyranids unit from your army is within Synapse Range of your army:

■ Each time that unit takes a Battle-shock test, take that test on 3D6 instead of 2D6.
■ Each time a model in that unit makes a melee attack, add 1 to the Strength characteristic of that attack.

glossary.name: Precision
glossary.effect: Weapons with [PRECISION] in their profile are known as Precision weapons. Each time an attack made with such a weapon successfully wounds an Attached unit, if a Character model in that unit is visible to the attacking model, the attacking model’s player can choose to have that attack allocated to that Character model instead of following the normal attack sequence.

glossary.name: Extra Attacks
glossary.effect: Weapons with [EXTRA ATTACKS] in their profile are known as Extra Attacks weapons. Each time the bearer of one or more Extra Attacks weapons fights, it makes attacks with each of the Extra Attacks melee weapons it is equipped with and it makes attacks with one of the melee weapons it is equipped with that does not have the [EXTRA ATTACKS] ability (if any). The number of attacks made with an Extra Attacks weapon cannot be modified by other rules, unless that weapon’s name is explicitly specified in that rule.

glossary.name: Blast
glossary.effect: Weapons with [BLAST] in their profile are known as Blast weapons, and they make a random number of attacks. Each time you determine how many attacks are made with a Blast weapon, add 1 to the result for every five models that were in the target unit when you selected it as the target (rounding down). Blast weapons can never be used to make attacks against a unit that is within Engagement Range of one or more units from the attacking model’s army (including its own unit).

glossary.name: Twin-linked
glossary.effect: Weapons with [TWIN-LINKED] in their profile are known as Twin-linked weapons. Each time an attack is made with such a weapon, you can re-roll that attack’s Wound roll.

glossary.name: Torrent
glossary.effect: Weapons with [TORRENT] in their profile are known as Torrent weapons. Each time an attack is made with such a weapon, that attack automatically hits the target.

glossary.name: Scouts
glossary.effect:
Some units have ‘Scouts x"’ listed in their abilities. If every model in a unit has this ability, then at the start of the first battle round, before the first turn begins, it can make a Normal move of up to x", with the exception that, while making that move, the distance moved by each model in that unit can be greater than that model's Move characteristic, as long as it is not greater than x". 

DEDICATED TRANSPORT models can make use of any Scouts x" ability listed in their abilities, or a Scouts x" ability that a unit that starts the battle embarked within that DEDICATED TRANSPORT  model has (provided only models with this ability are embarked within that Dedicated Transport model), regardless of how that embarked unit gained this ability (e.g. listed in their abilities, conferred by an Enhancement or by an attached Character, etc.). 

A unit that moves using this ability must end that move more than 9" horizontally away from all enemy models. If both players have units that can do this, the player who is taking the first turn moves their units first.

glossary.name: Devastating Wounds
glossary.effect: Weapons with [DEVASTATING WOUNDS] in their profile are known as Devastating Wounds weapons. Each time an attack is made with such a weapon, if that attack scores a Critical Wound, no saving throw of any kind can be made against that attack (including invulnerable saving throws). Such attacks are only allocated to models after all other attacks made by the attacking unit have been allocated and resolved. After that attack is allocated and after any modifiers are applied, it inflicts a number of mortal wounds on the target equal to the Damage characteristic of that attack, instead of inflicting damage normally.

glossary.name: Feel No Pain
glossary.effect: Some models have 'Feel No Pain x+' listed in their abilities. Each time a model with this ability suffers damage and so would lose a wound (including wounds lost due to mortal wounds), roll one D6: if the result is greater than or equal to the number denoted by 'x: that wound is ignored and is not lost. If a model has more than one Feel No Pain ability, you can only use one of those abilities each time that model suffers damage and so would lose a wound.

glossary.name: Assault
glossary.effect: Weapons with [ASSAULT] in their profile are known as Assault weapons. If a unit that Advanced this turn contains any models equipped with Assault weapons, it is still eligible to shoot in this turn’s Shooting phase. When such a unit is selected to shoot, you can only resolve attacks using Assault weapons its models are equipped with.

glossary.name: Pistol
glossary.effect:
Weapons with [PISTOL] in their profile are known as Pistols. If a unit contains any models equipped with Pistols, that unit is eligible to shoot in its controlling player’s Shooting phase even while it is within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units. When such a unit is selected to shoot, it can only resolve attacks using its Pistols and can only target one of the enemy units it is within Engagement Range of. In such circumstances, a Pistol can target an enemy unit even if other friendly units are within Engagement Range of the same enemy unit. 

If a model is equipped with one or more Pistols, unless it is a MONSTER or VEHICLE model, it can either shoot with its Pistols or with all of its other ranged weapons. Declare whether such a model will shoot with its Pistols or its other ranged weapons before selecting targets.

glossary.name: Anti-
glossary.effect: Weapons with [ANTI-KEYWORD X+] in their profile are known as Anti weapons. Each time an attack is made with such a weapon against a target with the keyword after the word ‘Anti-’, an unmodified Wound roll of ‘x+’ scores a Critical Wound.

glossary.name: Fights First
glossary.effect: Units with this ability that are eligible to fight do so in the Fights First step, provided every model in the unit has this ability.

glossary.name: Infiltrators
glossary.effect: During deployment, if every model in a unit has this ability, then when you set it up, it can be set up anywhere on the battlefield that is more than 9" horizontally away from the enemy deployment zone and all enemy models.

glossary.name: Lone Operative
glossary.effect: Unless part of an Attached unit, this unit can only be selected as the target of a ranged attack if the attacking model is within 12".

glossary.name: Stealth
glossary.effect: If every model in a unit has this ability, then each time a ranged attack is made against it, subtract 1 from that attack’s Hit roll.

glossary.name: Ignores Cover
glossary.effect: Weapons with [IGNORES COVER] in their profile are known as Ignores Cover weapons. Each time an attack is made with such a weapon, the target cannot have the Benefit of Cover against that attack.

glossary.name: Lethal Hits
glossary.effect: Weapons with [LETHAL HITS] in their profile are known as Lethal Hits weapons. Each time an attack is made with such a weapon, a Critical Hit automatically wounds the target.

glossary.name: Sustained Hits
glossary.effect: Weapons with [SUSTAINED HITS X] in their profile are known as Sustained Hits weapons. Each time an attack is made with such a weapon, if a Critical Hit is rolled, that attack scores a number of additional hits on the target as denoted by ‘x’

glossary.name: Heavy
glossary.effect: Weapons with [HEAVY] in their profile are known as Heavy weapons. Each time an attack is made with such a weapon, if the attacking model’s unit Remained Stationary this turn, add 1 to that attack’s Hit roll.

glossary.name: Indirect Fire
glossary.effect:
Weapons with [INDIRECT FIRE] in their profile are known as Indirect Fire weapons, and attacks can be made with them even if the target is not visible to the attacking model. These attacks can destroy enemy models in a target unit even though none may have been visible to the attacking unit when you selected that target.


If no models in a target unit are visible to the attacking unit when you select that target, then each time a model in the attacking unit makes an attack against that target using an Indirect Fire weapon, subtract 1 from that attack’s Hit roll, an unmodified Hit roll of 1-3 always fails, and the target has the Benefit of Cover against that attack. Weapons with the [TORRENT] ability cannot be fired using the [INDIRECT FIRE] ability.

glossary.name: Hover
glossary.effect:
Some AIRCRAFT models have 'Hover' listed in their abilities. When you are instructed to Declare Battle Formations, before doing anything else, you must first declare which models from your army with this ability will be in Hover mode.

If a model is in Hover mode,  then until the end of the battle, its Move characteristic is changed to 20", it loses the AIRCRAFT keyword and it loses all associated rules for being an AlRCRAFT model. Models in Hover mode do not start the battle in Reserves, but you can choose to place them into Strategic Reserves following the normal rules if you wish.

glossary.name: Leader
glossary.effect:
While a Bodyguard unit contains a Leader, it is known as an Attached unit and, with the exception of rules that are triggered when units are destroyed (pg 12), it is treated as a single unit for all rules purposes. Each time an attack targets an Attached unit, until the attacking unit has resolved all of its attacks, you must use the Toughness characteristic of the Bodyguard models in that unit, even if a Leader in that unit has a different Toughness characteristic. Each time an attack successfully wounds an Attached unit, that attack cannot be allocated to a Character model in that unit, even if that Character model has lost one or more wounds or has already had attacks allocated to it this phase. As soon as the last Bodyguard model in an Attached unit has been destroyed, any attacks made against that unit that have yet to be allocated can then be allocated to Character models in that unit.

Each time the last model in a Bodyguard unit is destroyed, each CHARACTER unit that is part of that Attached unit becomes a separate unit, with its original Starting Strength. If this happens as the result of an attack, they become separate units after the attacking unit has resolved all of its attacks. 

Each time the last model in a CHARACTER unit that is attached to a Bodyguard unit is destroyed and there is not another CHARACTER unit attached, that Attached unit’s Bodyguard unit becomes a separate unit, with its original Starting Strength. If this happens as the result of an attack, they become separate units after the attacking unit has resolved all of its attacks. 

Each time a unit that is part of an Attached unit is destroyed, it does not have the keywords of any other units that make up that Attached unit (unless it has those keywords on its own datasheet) for the purposes of any rules that would be triggered when that unit is destroyed.

glossary.name: Melta
glossary.effect: Weapons with [MELTA X] in their profile are known as Melta weapons. Each time an attack made with such a weapon targets a unit within half that weapon’s range, that attack’s Damage characteristic is increased by the amount denoted by ‘x’.

glossary.name: Psychic
glossary.effect: Some weapons and abilities can only be used by PSYKERS. Such weapons and abilities are tagged with the word 'Psychic'. If a Psychic weapon or ability causes any unit to suffer one or more wounds, each of those wounds is considered to have been inflicted by a Psychic Attack.
